prompt:
Add a new `build-android` job to `.github/workflows/test.yml` that builds a debug APK of the Capacitor Android wrapper on every push and PR. This is a smoke-test only — no signing, no release, no artifact distribution beyond a GitHub Actions workflow artifact.

**What to build:**
The flux repo now has a Capacitor Android wrapper under `android/` (appId `dev.gunk.flux`). The build flow is:
1. `npm ci` (installs Capacitor + Preact deps)
2. `npm run build` (Vite builds web bundle to `dist/`)
3. `npx cap sync android` (copies dist/ into android/ and updates plugin refs)
4. `cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug` (produces `android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`)

**Job spec — place after the existing `build` job, before `catalog-validation`:**

```yaml
build-android:
  name: Build Android (debug)
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  permissions:
    contents: read
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@<pinned-sha>  # match SHA used elsewhere in this file (v4)
      with:
        persist-credentials: false

    - name: Setup Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@<pinned-sha>  # latest v4, pin by SHA
      with:
        node-version: '20'

    - name: Setup JDK
      uses: actions/setup-java@<pinned-sha>  # latest v4, pin by SHA
      with:
        distribution: 'temurin'
        java-version: '17'

    - name: Setup Android SDK
      uses: android-actions/setup-android@<pinned-sha>  # latest v3, pin by SHA

    - name: Install deps and sync Capacitor
      run: |
        npm ci
        npm run build
        npx cap sync android

    - name: Build debug APK
      run: |
        cd android
        ./gradlew assembleDebug --no-daemon

    - name: Upload debug APK
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
      with:
        name: flux-debug-apk
        path: android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk
        retention-days: 7
```

**Important — pin every action by SHA.** The existing `test.yml` pins all actions by SHA with a version comment (`# v4`, `# v30`, etc.) — zizmor enforces this in CI and your PR will fail otherwise. Look up the latest stable release SHA for:
- `actions/checkout@v4` — reuse the SHA already used in `test.yml` (currently `34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5`)
- `actions/setup-node@v4`
- `actions/setup-java@v4`
- `android-actions/setup-android@v3`
Use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/latest --jq .tag_name,.target_commitish` if needed.

**Why this approach (not Nix-based):** flux's dev shell uses Nix for Node/Playwright, but adding the Android SDK to Nix is heavyweight and the dev shell doesn't currently include it. Use the standard GH actions for the Android toolchain — for a CI-only debug smoke test that's cleaner than expanding flake.nix. The Node parts (`npm ci`, `npm run build`) intentionally run via plain `actions/setup-node` here rather than through Nix to keep this job's toolchain self-contained.

**Validation before opening PR:**
- Run `nix run nixpkgs#nixfmt -- --check .` (the existing `nix-lint` job will check this).
- Run `nix run nixpkgs#zizmor -- . --no-online-audits` and confirm no new errors (warnings are OK — see the existing `zizmor` job's exit-code handling).
- You can't fully validate the Android build locally without the Android SDK, but you CAN sanity-check the gradle wrapper works: `cd android && ./gradlew tasks --no-daemon` (will likely fail without SDK, that's expected — just confirm the wrapper is executable and the build.gradle parses).

**Out of scope (these are tracked in separate issues, do not address):**
- Signed release builds, keystore handling, AAB, Play Store
- versionCode auto-increment (currently hardcoded to 1 in `android/app/build.gradle`)
- Adding Android SDK to flake.nix
- Caching gradle deps (can be added later if CI is slow)

**PR title:** "ci: add debug APK build job for Capacitor Android wrapper"
**PR body:** brief description + note that signed release pipeline + versionCode auto-increment are deferred to follow-up issues.
